back_translation: Brothers, do you not know — for I am speaking to those who know the Law — that the Law has authority over a human only as long as he still lives?
For a woman who has a husband is bound by the Law to her husband as long as the husband lives — but if the husband dies, she is released from the Law of the husband

Therefore, if she becomes the woman of another man while her husband is still alive, she will be called an adulteress — but if her husband dies, she is free from that Law, and she is not an adulteress when she becomes the woman of another man

Therefore, my brothers, you also have been put-to-death to the Law through the (royal) body of Christ — so that you may become of another, namely of the One who (royally) was raised from the dead, in order that we might bear fruit to God
For when we used to be in the flesh, the lusts of sins that were aroused by the Law worked in our body-parts, bearing fruit to death
But now, we have been released from the Law — for we have died to the thing that used to bind us — so that we may serve in the newness-of-life of the (royal) Spirit, not in the oldness of the letter
Therefore, what shall we say? Is the Law sin? Absolutely not — but I would not have known sin except through the Law; for I would not have known coveting if the Law had not said, 'Do not covet'
But sin seized the opportunity through the (royal) commandment, producing every kind of coveting in me — for if there is no Law, sin lies dead
In the past, I used to live without the Law — but when the (royal) commandment came, sin sprang back to life
And I died — and I discovered that the (royal) commandment, which was intended for life, instead brought death
For sin seized the opportunity through the (royal) commandment, deceived me, and killed me through that (royal) commandment
Therefore, the Law is holy, and the (royal) commandment is also holy, righteous, and good

Therefore, did the good thing become death to me? Absolutely not — but it is sin, so that sin would appear as sin, by using the good thing to produce death in me — so that sin might become sin in excess, through the (royal) commandment
For we know that the Law is spiritual — but I am of the flesh, having been sold to be under sin
For I do not understand what I myself do — for what I desire, I do not do — but what I hate, I instead do
And if I do what I do not desire, I admit that the Law is good
In that case, it is no longer I myself who do it any more — but it is sin that dwells in me
For I know that — in me, that is, in my flesh — there is nothing good that dwells; for desiring the good is in me, but doing the good to completion is not
For the good that I desire, I do not do — but the evil that I do not desire, I instead do
And if I do what I do not desire, then it is no longer I myself who do it any more — but it is sin that dwells in me
Therefore, I find this principle — when I desire to do the good, evil is near me
For I joyfully rejoice in the Law of God according to the inner human
But I see another principle in my body-parts — making war against the principle of my mind, and taking me captive in the principle of sin which is in my body-parts
I am a person miserable in the extreme! Who will rescue me from this body of death?
Thanks be to God, through Jesus Christ our Lord! Therefore, I myself serve the Law of God with the mind — but serve the principle of sin with the flesh
